title: gentiana amarella var. pilosa
intro: Gentiana amarella var. pilosa is a variety of the Gentian family of plants, also known as the hairy or downy gentian. This perennial herb is native to North America, valued for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant contains compounds like gentiopicrin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Gentiana amarella var. pilosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and it is also used in herbal preparations for its digestive and respiratory health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function.
